text: Israel is preparing possible strikes on Iranian energy sites but is waiting for U.S. approval. A decision could come within a week. Source: Reuters
WATCH: Hezbollah FPV drones strike two Israeli army tanks.

U.S. intelligence says Iran is using decoys, making it hard to tell which targets are real. This creates uncertainty about how many actual missile systems have been destroyed, since some strikes may have hit fakes instead of real launchers. Source: NYT

Top advisers around Trump have been privately arguing that Iran’s infrastructure—like power plants and road networks—can be treated as legitimate military targets. Pete Hegseth and others have told Trump that striking these systems could disrupt Iran’s missile and drone capabilities, and even create internal instability that slows its nuclear progress. Source: WSJ

Iran's Tasnim: U.S. forces are bombing areas in the southwest of the country where their missing pilot is believed to be located. They are attempting to kill their pilot after losing hope of finding him.

⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 Commander of the Khatam-al Anbiya Central Headquarters, Major General Ali Abdollahi Aliabadi, warned that the armed forces will put any "aggressor" in its place. He described U.S. President Trump’s threats against Iranian infrastructure as the desperate act of a failing, unbalanced and foolish leader. Abdollahi said that if the U.S. and Israel attack, Iran will strike all US military infrastructure in the region and Israeli infrastructure without limitation. “The gates of hell will be opened on you,” he said. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 🇺🇳 Iran’s UN ambassador, Amir Saeid Iravani, has written to the UN Secretary-General and other international bodies, condemning U.S. and Israeli attacks on the B1 bridge in Karaj and the Mahshahr petrochemical plant as war crimes and state terrorism. He stated that the deliberate and systematic targeting of civilians and infrastructure essential for civilian survival—including power, water and energy facilities—constitutes a grave violation of international humanitarian law and is intended to spread "terror" and inflict widespread suffering. @wfwitness

The Iraqi people's national demand from Al-Sudani's government: Take back Iraq's skies from America ▪️ Exclusive report by Tasnim correspondent in Baghdad (via Tasnim News ) 🔴 The Iraqi people believe their country's skies have been turned into an open corridor for American and Israeli warplanes and drones. These aircraft not only carry out attacks on Iran but also bomb the centers and headquarters of the Islamic Resistance in Iraq , particularly the Popular Mobilization Forces ( Al-Hashd Al-Shaabi ). ➡️ Sabah Zanganeh, head of the Iraqi Center for Strategic Studies , said: "This issue directly undermines Iraq's sovereignty and territorial integrity, and clearly shows that the Iraqi government's authority over its own airspace has been weakened." ➡️ What stings most for every Iraqi citizen is the feeling that, in the face of this violation of their nation's sovereignty, they can do nothing but condemn it and express their disgust. ➡️ That may explain why, in recent weeks, pressure has mounted on Iraqi officials—especially Prime Minister Mohammed Shia' Al-Sudani —to take concrete measures to end this situation. ➡️ At the same time, the Iraqi Parliament has called for revising the current security agreement between Baghdad and Washington and has urged the purchase of powerful, effective air defense systems from other countries, such as Russia , China , and South Korea . 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

🇺🇸 Houston, We Have a Smell Now halfway to the Moon, the Artemis II crew has reported their second toilet issue of the mission. Astronauts say there is a burning, heater-like smell coming from the toilet, though mission control is not concerned. The first problem with the $23 million Orion space toilet was reported just hours after launch, when a fan was found to be jammed. The crew was able to fix the issue with guidance from mission control. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics
